title: Commending efforts to eradicate the wild poliovirus.
plain_summary: This resolution supports efforts to get rid of polio around the world. Polio is a serious disease that can make people very sick and unable to walk. Since 1988, groups working together have almost wiped out polio completely, with cases going down by more than 99 percent. Only two countries, Afghanistan and Pakistan, still have people getting sick from polio. The House of Representatives wants to keep supporting the work to make polio disappear everywhere.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution praises efforts to eliminate the wild poliovirus, which causes polio.

sections.title: Historical Background
sections.text: In nineteen fifty-two, almost sixty thousand children in the United States got polio. More than twenty thousand of these children became paralyzed. Thanks to vaccines, polio was completely eliminated from the United States by nineteen seventy-nine.

sections.title: Global Polio Eradication Initiative
sections.text: The Global Polio Eradication Initiative is a partnership between government and private groups that started in nineteen eighty-eight. This partnership includes the Federal Government, Rotary International, the Gates Foundation, and GAVI. The goal was to eliminate polio everywhere in the world. When they started, there were more than three hundred fifty thousand cases of polio recorded each year.

sections.title: Rotary International Contribution
sections.text: Rotary International is a worldwide organization that was founded in Illinois. Since nineteen seventy-nine, Rotary has given more than two billion eight hundred million dollars to fight polio around the world. They have also volunteered countless hours of work.

sections.title: Progress Made
sections.text: Since the goal to eliminate polio worldwide was set in nineteen eighty-eight, polio cases have been reduced by more than ninety-nine point nine percent. This happened through the use of two vaccines. One vaccine was created by Jonas Salk and the other was created by Albert Sabin.

sections.title: Regional Certification
sections.text: The Americas were the first region to be declared free from wild poliovirus in nineteen ninety-four. The last case of wild poliovirus in the Americas was confirmed in Peru in nineteen ninety-one. Since nineteen ninety-four, four other regions of the world have been declared free from wild poliovirus. Two of the three types of wild poliovirus have been completely eliminated worldwide.

sections.title: Remaining Challenges
sections.text: As of September twenty twenty-four, only Afghanistan and Pakistan have been unable to completely stop the spread of wild poliovirus. This is a big improvement from more than one hundred twenty-five countries in nineteen eighty-eight. The Global Polio Eradication Initiative works to reach the world's most vulnerable children. This includes children who have been forced to move from their homes and those living in dangerous areas or places with conflict, where different types of poliovirus are still a concern.

sections.title: Lives Saved
sections.text: Today, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, an estimated twenty million people are walking who would have been paralyzed. One million five hundred thousand deaths have been prevented worldwide thanks to the work of the Global Polio Eradication Initiative.

sections.title: Additional Benefits
sections.text: Besides fighting polio, the worldwide workforce and systems of the Global Polio Eradication Initiative have often served as first responders for natural disasters and other public health crises. This includes outbreaks of Ebola and the COVID-19 pandemic.

sections.title: Global Collaboration
sections.text: Progress toward eliminating polio worldwide shows what can be achieved through continued global commitment and working together. When polio is eliminated, it will be only the second human disease in history to be completely wiped out. The first was smallpox.

sections.title: Current Work
sections.text: The Global Polio Eradication Initiative effectively reaches children to protect them from polio even in unstable countries affected by conflict. This includes most recently in Palestine.

sections.title: Ongoing Threat
sections.text: Countries that have been considered polio-free for a long time, including the United States, are still at risk of polio outbreaks until poliovirus transmission is stopped everywhere in the world. The United States confirmed a case of polio in twenty twenty-two in the State of New York. As long as poliovirus spreads anywhere, it remains a threat to children everywhere.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: Therefore, the House of Representatives does the following four things. First, it praises the work that has kept the Americas free from wild poliovirus. Second, it supports the goals and ideals of the Global Polio Eradication Initiative. Third, it encourages and supports governments and nongovernmental organizations around the world to remain committed to eliminating polio. Fourth, it encourages the Federal Government to continue providing funding to the Global Polio Eradication Initiative.
